page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate a balanced profile for multi-threaded operations. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests is positioned in the bottom 10%, multi-core CPU performance scales to average levels. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show a distinct contrast: small-block cached read operations perform at average levels, whereas large-block uncached read and write operations are weaker, falling into the bottom 25%. Standard processing tasks, such as gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Passmark CPU instruction tests, consistently register average performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer a predictable compute-to-memory balance without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes the instance suitable for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local SSD storage. Recommended workloads include multi-threaded batch processing, static web serving, Redis database deployments, and general-purpose computational tasks that can leverage the 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz, the server is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.0 GB of memory per core. It contains no local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates with an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals average multi-core CPU performance and average small-block cached memory bandwidth, though uncached large-block memory throughput is weaker. The dedicated CPU allocation makes this instance cost-efficient for compute-heavy, multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and parallel batch processing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated thirty-two core AMD EPYC compute instance designed for multi-threaded processing and balanced memory-to-CPU workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core CPU and cached memory read performance
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for static web serving, Redis, and multi-threaded batch processing
